commands.repair_reason: - The remaining blocker is maintainer product/RFC acceptance, not a concrete automated patch.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff adds one Markdown RFC and does not touch executable code, workflows, dependencies, secrets, or release automation. commands.review_summary:
Adds a new draft RFC Markdown file proposing an opt-in SQLite snapshot plugin, provider contract, `openclaw snapshot` commands, hosted artifact boundary, and staged implementation roadmap.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds RFC text and does not report a broken runtime behavior path. The relevant checks are document review, proof freshness, and RFC lifecycle state.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blocker is maintainer product/RFC acceptance, not a concrete automated patch.

target.title: RFC 0013: SQLite State Snapshot Plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- Proposes RFC 0013, SQLite State Snapshot Plugin.
- Makes the hosted persistence problem visceral: `state/openclaw.sqlite` and `agents/<agentId>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite` are not safe host-sync inputs by themselves when committed writes may still live in `*.sqlite-wal`; `*.sqlite-wal`, `*.sqlite-shm`, and `*.sqlite-journal` are process-local sidecars, not durable artifacts.
- Defines the sync mechanism: OpenClaw materializes a clean SQLite artifact in staging, verifies it, then writes the final artifact + manifest into a sync-owned artifact directory; that completed file write is what should trigger Lobster/Aether-style host sync.
- Adds the hosted OpenClaw need explicitly: OpenClaw provides the state-artifact boundary from live local SQLite DBs to verified artifacts, while hosts own upload, retention, routing, encryption, and restore timing.
- Keeps SQLite as the hot local runtime database and makes snapshot/restore an opt-in plugin extension.
- Defines a reusable SQLite snapshot provider contract for OpenClaw-owned SQLite databases.
- Keeps the initial implementation stack scoped to `openclaw snapshot`, with `openclaw backup` integration left as possible later work.
- Avoids choosing PostgreSQL, object storage, or any replacement backend in this RFC.

## Roadmap

1. Provider proof: bundled `extensions/snapshot` scaffold, local SQLite snapshot provider, manifest/hash verification, and WAL-mode SQLite tests with internal restore proof.
2. Public snapshot CLI: `openclaw snapshot create`, `openclaw snapshot verify`, and `openclaw snapshot restore` with restore safety and docs.
3. Fresh-state boot proof: hydrate a fresh OpenClaw state dir from a restored snapshot before runtime opens SQLite, and document the minimum host-facing command/API contract.

## Scope

This is an RFC only. It does not change OpenClaw runtime behavior, add storage configuration, or implement backup/restore commands.

## Validation

- Draft follows `rfcs/0000-template.md` shape.
- Related issue context checked: openclaw/openclaw#90370, #94675, and #88838.
- Database-first target model checked: openclaw/openclaw#94646.
- Current OpenClaw CLI/docs surface checked: `openclaw backup create` and `openclaw backup verify` exist; no restore/snapshot/failover command currently owns this workflow.
- Bundled plugin comparison checked: `policy` and `oc-path` use `extensions/<plugin>` manifests and command aliases.
- `git diff --check` passed at head `b36b20af152c025542259ff720719e1a0e720bee`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR adds the draft RFC text for SQLite-safe state snapshots. The visible behavior is the final RFC document rendered by GitHub from the PR head.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout of `openclaw/rfcs` at PR head `b36b20af152c025542259ff720719e1a0e720bee`, using Git/GitHub CLI terminal checks. This is documentation/RFC proof, not runtime OpenClaw behavior proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git rev-parse HEAD`
2. `git show --stat --oneline --no-renames HEAD`
3. `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
4. Read `rfcs/0013-cloud-serializable-sqlite-state.md` frontmatter and line count.

Evidence after fix:
```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
b36b20af152c025542259ff720719e1a0e720bee

$ git show --stat --oneline --no-renames HEAD
b36b20a clarify syncable sqlite artifact creation
 rfcs/0013-cloud-serializable-sqlite-state.md | 41 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 41 insertions(+)

$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# no output

file=rfcs/0013-cloud-serializable-sqlite-state.md
frontmatter:
title: SQLite State Snapshot Plugin
authors:
  - giodl
created: 2026-06-18
last_updated: 2026-06-19
status: draft
issue:
rfc_pr: https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/20
```

Observed result after fix:
The final PR head contains one RFC Markdown file with draft metadata, the RFC PR link, explicit hosted-state artifact boundary language, unsafe SQLite sync input examples, and no whitespace errors from `git diff --check`. The implementation issue remains intentionally blank because the RFC has not been accepted.

What was not tested:
No runtime OpenClaw behavior was tested because this PR is RFC text only. Maintainer discussion thread creation is still pending in Discord `maintainer-discussion`, which requires maintainer-side Discord action/access. Acceptance metadata (`status: accepted` and `issue`) is intentionally not set while the RFC remains draft.

## RFC Lifecycle

- Status: draft
- Implementation issue: intentionally blank until acceptance
- Maintainer discussion thread: pending maintainer-side Discord thread in `maintainer-discussion`
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
